frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Maa ka pyaar, maa ka khana... duniya mein iski koi jodi nahi. Yaad hai kaise meri Ammi subah se shaam tak hum sabke liye lagi rehti thi. Kabhi thakti nahi thi, bas pyar se banati rehti thi sab. Woh swaad, woh khushboo... aaj bhi dil mein basi hai. Koi kitna bhi achcha bana le, Ammi ke haath jaisa pyar toh koi de hi nahi sakta. Asli mohabbat toh tabhi dikhti hai jab koi bina bole, sabki khushi ke liye mehnat karta hai. 🙏
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment glorifies the mother's tireless domestic labor, specifically cooking, as the ultimate expression of love and sacrifice ('bina bole, sabki khushi ke liye mehnat karta hai'). It implicitly sets this as an unmatched standard ('Ammi ke haath jaisa pyar toh koi de hi nahi sakta'), erasing the labor involved by framing it solely as love, and creating an implicit comparison that devalues the efforts of other women (e.g., wife, daughters) who might not meet this idealized standard of selfless domestic service.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Tanveer. Age 42. Married. A Muslim man in Bhopal. You have four children Ñ two sons and two daughters. Both sons are in college. Your elder daughter who is 19 wants to go to college too. You have been thinking about it. You are not opposed to education in principle. But you are talking to your wife tonight about the specific concerns you have about sending your daughter to a mixed college where there are boys, where the environment is open, where she will be exposed to ideas and people that are outside your community and your values. You are telling your wife what you have decided and how you are going to explain it to your daughter.
